beats.activity: Evening leisure: journal, review camera settings, and read with a calm mindset.
beats.reasoning: Jack's intentional wind-down routine; he values solitude and reflection. Camera passion and journaling continue yesterday's grounded tone.
7-hour overnight sleep, ending at 6am Saturday. Jack recharges after a solid week and warm Friday to prepare for weekend adventures (fly fishing, photography).

diary:
Woke early in that perfect quiet before the city stirs—coffee, stretches, the usual ritual that centers me. The morning classes flowed well; there's something about Friday energy that gets people really present on their mats. Grabbed a quick bite at Voula's between sessions and felt that familiar hum of a full teaching day.

Drove home early to dodge the Pride crowds gathering downtown. Smart call. Instead of fighting traffic, I spent the golden afternoon in my studio, painting. The light was that particular warm Seattle gold you only get a few times a year, and I just let the brush move. By evening I was content on the couch—journaling, tinkering with camera settings, reading. Felt genuinely settled. The city's out there celebrating, and here I am in this pocket of calm, and that feels exactly right.
thought_process: I prioritized the morning routine because that's my anchor—without it, the rest of the day feels untethered. Teaching three classes meant staying grounded and present with each group; I didn't want to phone it in. When I realized the Pride closures would clog my usual route home, I saw it as an opportunity rather than an obstacle. Instead of sitting in traffic frustrated, I could claim back real time. The afternoon painting session wasn't random—I was riding the energy of that warm day and needed creative expression after being "on" all morning. By evening, I deliberately chose quiet over going out. Not because Pride doesn't matter to me, but because my nervous system needed restoration. The day felt balanced: service, then solitude.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
